N.description: Number of existing hubs that must remain open

M.description: Total number of hub locations (existing and potential)

v.description: Commuter demand from each junction to each POI

c_sp.description: Car travel time from each junction to each POI (minutes)

c_sh.description: Car travel time from each junction to each hub (minutes)

b_hp.description: Bike travel time from each hub to each POI (minutes)

d_sp.description: Driving distance from each junction to each POI (kilometers)

d_hp.description: Distance from each hub to each POI (kilometers)

d_sh.description: Driving distance from each junction to each hub (kilometers)

T.description: Maximum allowed bike travel time from a hub to a POI (minutes)

U.description: Maximum number of new hubs that can be opened

D.description: Minimum required distance between a hub and a POI (kilometers)

Delta.description: Maximum additional travel time allowed when using a bike via a hub compared to driving directly by car (minutes)

tau.description: Minimum required distance saving when routing via a hub (kilometers)

notes: We add implicit assumptions that all sets are non-empty, $U, v_{sp} \geq 0$ and $N \leq M$.
